perturbation.original_text: f) "Program Member(s)" shall mean an Excite@Home Member who has: Source: INVENDA CORP, S-1/A, 8/28/2000 i) Been provided access to the Co-Branded Application, and ii) Has opted-in to allow all his or her registration information to be shared with Application Provider and/or unspecified third parties. iii) Each Program Member shall be further classified into one of two mutually exclusive categories, General Program Member or Qualified Program Member, as defined below: (1) "General Program Member(s)" shall mean a Program Member who has not provided "Shopping Category Data" about him or herself, as defined below, and whose URS Data has been provided to e-centives no later than 5 days after the original date the User became an Excite@Home Member. (2) "Qualified Program Member(s)" shall mean a Program Member who has provided "Shopping Category Data" about him or herself, as defined below.
perturbation.changed_text: f) "Program Member(s)" shall mean an Excite@Home Member who has: Source: INVENDA CORP, S-1/A, 8/28/2000 i) Been provided access to the Co-Branded Application, and ii) Has opted-in to allow all his or her registration information to be shared with Application Provider and/or unspecified third parties. iii) Each Program Member shall be classified as a Program Member.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the classification of Program Members into 'General' and 'Qualified' categories, and also their definitions, the agreement now lacks clarity on how these members are segmented. The subsequent payment structure, which relies heavily on this classification, becomes unenforceable. Section 7, which outlines payments based on 'General Program Member User Data records' and 'Qualified Program Member User Data records', now has an in-text contradiction because the foundational definitions are missing.

perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the definitions of 'General Program Member' and 'Qualified Program Member'. Section 7 explicitly refers to these categories when outlining payment terms and delivery schedules for User Data records. By removing the definitions, the agreement now lacks clarity on which records fall into each category, making it impossible to determine the correct payment amount for each type of User Data record as described in section 7, creating a direct in-text contradiction and unenforceability issue.

perturbation.original_text: i) Excite@Home shall not offer any Excite-branded or Excite-co-branded service during the Term of this Agreement that is substantially similar in functionally to the Co-Branded Application.
perturbation.changed_text: i) Excite@Home may offer any Excite-branded or Excite-co-branded service during the Term of this Agreement that is substantially similar in functionally to the Co-Branded Application.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall not' to 'may', Excite@Home is now permitted to launch competing services, directly contradicting the implied exclusivity that underpins the agreement and e-centives' investment. This contradicts the overall intent of the Co-Branding Agreement, as it undermines the purpose of e-centives co-branding with Excite@Home's services, making the whole Co-Branding agreement non-exclusive to E-centives.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. CO-BRANDED APPLICATION a) Application Provider will, at its sole expense, develop, host and maintain the Co-Branded Application for Excite@Home. The primary function of the Co-Branded Application will be to present Users with coupons or other offers (both targeted and untargeted, as hereinafter defined in EXHIBIT A) for products and services provided by Source: INVENDA CORP, S-1/A, 8/28/2000 Application Provider's merchant partners with the intent of converting said Users into Program 3 4 *****Confidential Treatment has been requested for portions of this agreement. The copy file herewith omits information subject to the confidentiality request. Omissions are designated as [*****]. A complete version of this agreement has been filed separately with the Securities and Exchange Commission. Members. The Co-Branded Application will include offers and other content and functionality (Excite Content and e-centives Content, collectively "Application Content") as further described in EXHIBIT B.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits Excite@Home from offering services functionally similar to the Co-Branded Application, thereby implying exclusivity for E-centives. The changed text, by allowing Excite@Home to offer competing services, directly undermines the 'primary function' of the Co-Branded Application as stated in the contradicted text (Section 2a), which is to present users with coupons and offers from E-centives merchant partners. This creates a direct conflict because Excite@Home could now compete with its own co-branded application, reducing the value of the co-branding agreement for E-centives and contradicting the intent of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 16. TERMINATION a) Basis for Termination: i) Either party may terminate if the other party breaches the Agreement and the breach remains uncured for thirty (30) days following receipt of written notice of intention to terminate from the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the Basis of Termination, The agreement becomes perpetually binding unless both parties mutually agree to end it, which creates a significant imbalance and uncertainty, The document still refers to termination effects in subsequent clauses, thus making the removal of the initial termination cause, an in-text contradiction. This creates uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16. TERMINATION a) Basis for Termination: v) In the event that a majority of Application Provider's assets are merged, acquired or sold to an Excite@Home Named Competitor, or to an entity controlling or controlled by an Excite@Home Named Competitor, then Excite@Home may terminate this Agreement by providing thirty (30) days written notice. This option to terminate this Agreement may only be exercised by providing written notice within ninety (90) days of Excite@Home's receiving notice of such transaction. Application Provider Source: INVENDA CORP, S-1/A, 8/28/2000 shall provide such notice at least twenty-four (24) hours before the public announcement of any such transaction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the basis for termination, while the contradicted text refers to specific termination scenarios based on those removed bases. This creates a contradiction because the agreement now lacks a clear mechanism for termination except for the scenario outlined in the contradicted text, making the entire Termination clause inconsistent and unclear. Specifically, by removing the original clause 'Either party may terminate if the other party breaches the Agreement', a conflict arises as the contradicted text assumes there is a basis for termination that no longer exists. This directly affects the enforceability and interpretation of the termination clause, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.
